session.user:
Edit these docs files to replace printing/5-file/5-place references. The printing.py gateway variant has been removed from the codebase. The pattern is now: - 3-file (default): abc.py, real.py, fake.py - 4-file (opt-in for dry-run): abc.py, real.py, fake.py, dry_run.py
Rules: - Replace "5-file" with "4-file" when referring to dry-run-enabled gateways - Replace "5-place" with "4-place" when referring to dry-run-enabled gateways - Remove "printing.py" from lists of files - Remove references to "Printing*" classes - When a list says "abc.py, real.py, fake.py, dry_run.py, printing.py" change to "abc.py, real.py, fake.py, dry_run.py"
Read each file first, then edit.
1. `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/testing/monkeypatch-elimination-checklist.md` - Line 45: "5-file (or 3-file simplified) pattern" → "4-file (or 3-file simplified) pattern"
2. `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/testing/frozen-dataclass-test-doubles.md` - Line 88: "the 5-file pattern" → "the 4-file pattern"
3. `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/testing/gateway-fake-testing-exemplar.md` - Line 89: "The 5-place implementation pattern" → "The 4-place implementation pattern"
4. `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/uncategorized/tripwires.md` - Line 19: "Must implement in 5 places: abc.py, real.py, fake.py, dry_run.py, printing.py" → "Must implement in 4 places: abc.py, real.py, fake.py, dry_run.py"
5. `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/planning/gateway-consolidation-checklist.md` - Line 25: "5-file pattern (abc/real/fake/dry_run/printing)" → "4-file pattern (abc/real/fake/dry_run)" - Line 28: "full 5-file pattern" → "full 4-file pattern"; "3-file pattern (no dry_run/printing)" → "3-file pattern (no dry_run)" - Line 86: "5-place implementation pattern" → "4-place implementation pattern"
6. `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/gateway/codespace-gateway.md` - Line 21: "5-place gateway pattern" → "4-place gateway pattern"; remove "and printing" from the sentence about omitting them - Line 27: "5-place (standard)" → "4-place (standard)" - Line 80: "5-place pattern, 3-place variant decision criteria" → "4-place pattern, 3-place variant decision criteria"
7. `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/universal-tripwires.md` - Line 12: "Must implement in 5 places: abc.py, real.py, fake.py, dry_run.py, printing.py." → "Must implement in 4 places: abc.py, real.py, fake.py, dry_run.py." - Line 44: "5-place implementation pattern" → "4-place implementation pattern"
8. `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/refactoring/libcst-systematic-imports.md` - Line 380: "the 5-place pattern" → "the 4-place pattern"
9. `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/cli/incomplete-command-removal.md` - Line 47: "5-place pattern" → "4-place pattern"
10. `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/tui/view-switching.md` - Line 135: "5-place gateway pattern" → "gateway pattern" (this reference is about fetch_plan_content which uses a different count; just remove the "5-place" qualifier)
11. `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/glossary.md` - Line 646: "5-file implementation pattern: abc.py, real.py, fake.py, dry_run.py, printing.py" → "implementation pattern: abc.py, real.py, fake.py (3-file default), plus dry_run.py for dry-run-enabled gateways (4-file)"
12. `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/reviews/development.md` - Read the file first. If it contains printing/5-file references, update them. If not, skip it.
Read each file first, then make the edit. Do ALL files.
session.assistant: I'll read each file first and then make the necessary edits to replace printing/5-file/5-place references with the updated 3-file/4-file pattern.
session.tool_result:
1→--- 2→title: Monkeypatch Elimination Checklist 3→read_when: 4→ - "migrating tests from monkeypatch to gateways" 5→ - "eliminating subprocess mocks or @patch decorators" 6→ - "encountering monkeypatch in existing tests and deciding whether to refactor" 7→tripwires: 8→ - action: "adding monkeypatch or @patch to a test" 9→ warning: "Use gateway fakes instead. If no gateway exists for the operation, create one first. See gateway-abc-implementation.md." 10→ pattern: "@patch|monkeypatch\\." 11→ - action: "using monkeypatch to stub Path.home() or subprocess.run()" 12→ warning: "These are the two most common monkeypatch targets. Both have established gateway replacements — ClaudeInstallation/ErkInstallation for paths, specific gateways for subprocess." 13→ pattern: "monkeypatch\\.setattr.*Path\\.home|monkeypatch.*subprocess\\.run" 14→last_audited: "2026-02-16 14:20 PT" 15→audit_result: clean 16→--- 17→ 18→# Monkeypatch Elimination Checklist 19→ 20→## Why Monkeypatch Is Especially Harmful in Erk 21→ 22→Erk's gateway ABC pattern means every external operation has a type-checked fake. Monkeypatching bypasses this entire system, creating tests that: 23→ 24→- **Couple to implementation, not interface** — a monkeypatch on `subprocess.run(["gh", ...])` breaks when the gateway switches from subprocess to the REST API, even though the behavior is identical 25→- **Evade the type checker** — patching attributes that don't exist on the ABC silently succeeds, then silently lies about coverage 26→- **Fragment test setup** — each test reinvents its own mock configuration instead of reusing constructor-injected fakes that are centralized and maintained 27→ 28→The gateway fakes already handle the hard parts (configurable success/failure, mutation tracking, assertion helpers). Monkeypatching is always duplicating work that's already done better. 29→ 30→## Mapping Mocked Operations to Gateways
... [86 lines omitted] ...

1→--- 2→title: Frozen Dataclass Test Doubles 3→read_when: 4→ - "implementing a fake for an ABC interface" 5→ - "adding mutation tracking to a test double" 6→ - "choosing between frozen dataclass fakes and __init__-based fakes" 7→ - "writing tests that assert on method call parameters" 8→tripwires: 9→ - action: "exposing a mutation tracking list directly as a property without copying" 10→ warning: "Return list(self._tracked_list) from properties, not self._tracked_list. Direct exposure lets test code accidentally mutate the tracking state." 11→ - action: "tracking only the primary argument in a mutation tuple, omitting flags or options" 12→ warning: "Track ALL call parameters in tuples (e.g., (branch, force) not just branch). Lost context leads to undertested behavior." 13→ - action: "creating a fake that uses __init__ when frozen dataclass would work" 14→ warning: "FakeBranchManager uses frozen dataclass because its state is simple and declarative. FakeGitHub uses __init__ because it has 30+ constructor params. Choose based on complexity." 15→last_audited: "2026-02-08 00:00 PT" 16→audit_result: edited 17→--- 18→ 19→# Frozen Dataclass Test Doubles 20→ 21→Erk's gateway fakes use two distinct patterns for mutation tracking: frozen dataclasses with mutable internals (for simpler fakes) and `__init__`-based classes (for complex fakes). Both patterns serve the same purpose — recording method calls so tests can assert on what happened — but the choice between them has architectural consequences. 22→ 23→## Why Two Patterns Exist 24→ 25→The codebase uses frozen dataclass fakes and `__init__`-based fakes for different situations. The split isn't arbitrary — it reflects a complexity threshold. 26→ 27→<!-- Source: packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/branch_manager/fake.py, FakeBranchManager --> 28→ 29→`FakeBranchManager` uses `@dataclass(frozen=True)` because it has a manageable number of fields (around 15), all with `field(default_factory=...)` defaults. The frozen constraint provides a useful guarantee: field references can't be reassigned after construction, so a mutation tracking list can't be accidentally replaced. 30→
... [65 lines omitted] ...
39→| Field reassignment protection | Yes (`FrozenInstanceError`) | No | 46→This means frozen fakes can track mutations by appending to internal lists while still preventing accidental field replacement. A test can't do `fake._deleted_branches = []` (raises `FrozenInstanceError`), but the fake's own methods can do `self._deleted_branches.append(...)`.

1→--- 2→title: Gateway Fake Testing Exemplar 3→read_when: 4→ - "writing tests for gateway fakes that return discriminated unions" 5→ - "deciding whether mutation tracking should occur on error paths" 6→ - "implementing a new fake gateway method with error injection" 7→tripwires: 8→ - action: "creating a fake gateway without constructor-injected error configuration" 9→ warning: "Fakes must accept error variants at construction time (e.g., push_to_remote_error=PushError(...)) to enable failure injection in tests." 10→ - action: "using truthiness checks or .success on discriminated union results in tests" 11→ warning: "Always use isinstance() for type narrowing. Never check bool(result) or result.success — these bypass the type system." 12→ - action: "tracking mutations before checking error configuration in a fake method" 13→ warning: "Decide deliberately: should this operation track even on failure? push_to_remote skips tracking on error (nothing happened), pull_rebase always tracks (the attempt matters). Match the real operation's semantics." 14→--- 15→ 16→# Gateway Fake Testing Exemplar 17→ 18→This document captures the cross-cutting design decisions in fake gateway testing — specifically, the three patterns that every fake test must exercise and the subtle tracking-on-error decision that differs between operations. 19→ 20→## Why Fakes Need Their Own Tests 21→ 22→Fakes are production code for tests. A broken fake silently produces false-positive test results across the entire test suite. The `tests/unit/fakes/` directory exists to verify that fakes honor their ABC contracts and that their error-injection and mutation-tracking machinery works correctly. 23→ 24→<!-- Source: tests/unit/fakes/test_fake_git_remote_ops.py --> 25→ 26→See `test_fake_git_remote_ops.py` in `tests/unit/fakes/` for the canonical exemplar. 27→ 28→## Three Required Test Categories Per Operation 29→ 30→Every fake method that returns a discriminated union needs three test categories:
... [66 lines omitted] ...
35→| **Error injection** | Constructor-configured error is returned | Proves failure paths work without modifying fake internals | 40→## The Tracking-on-Error Decision 42→The most subtle design choice in fake implementation is whether mutation tracking should occur when an error is configured. This is not arbitrary — it follows the real operation's semantics: 44→| Operation | Tracks on error? | Rationale | 52→Compare `FakeGitRemoteOps.push_to_remote()` (early-returns error before tracking) with `FakeGitRemoteOps.pull_rebase()` (tracks first, then checks error) in `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/git/remote_ops/fake.py`. 54→**When implementing a new fake method**, ask: "If the real operation fails, did a side effect still occur?" If yes, track before checking the error. If no, check the error first and skip tracking. 81→if isinstance(result, PushError): 85→**Testing only the happy path:** Every discriminated union method needs both success and error tests. A fake that only tests success could silently break error injection for all consumers. 90→- [Discriminated Union Error Handling](../architecture/discriminated-union-error-handling.md) — Design decisions for when to use unions vs exceptions

1→--- 2→title: Uncategorized Tripwires 3→read_when: 4→ - "working on uncategorized code" 5→--- 6→ 7→<!-- AUTO-GENERATED FILE - DO NOT EDIT DIRECTLY --> 8→<!-- Edit source frontmatter, then run 'erk docs sync' to regenerate. --> 9→<!-- Generated from uncategorized/*.md frontmatter --> 10→ 11→# Uncategorized Tripwires 12→ 13→Rules triggered by matching actions in code. 14→ 15→**adding --force flag to a CLI command** → Read [Code Conventions](conventions.md) first. Always include -f as the short form. Pattern: @click.option("-f", "--force", ...) 16→ 17→**adding a function with 5+ parameters** → Read [Code Conventions](conventions.md) first. Load `dignified-python` skill first. Use keyword-only arguments (add `*` after first param). Exception: ABC/Protocol method signatures and Click command callbacks. 18→ 19→**adding a new method to Git/GitHub/Graphite ABC** → Read [Universal Tripwires](universal-tripwires.md) first. Must implement in 5 places: abc.py, real.py, fake.py, dry_run.py, printing.py. 20→ 21→**adding file I/O or subprocess calls to class `__init__`** → Read [Universal Tripwires](universal-tripwires.md) first. Keep `__init__` lightweight; use factory methods like `from_config_path()`. 22→ 23→**calling os.chdir() in erk code** → Read [Universal Tripwires](universal-tripwires.md) first. After os.chdir(), regenerate context using regenerate_context(). 24→ 25→**editing source files directly on master branch** → Read [Code Conventions](conventions.md) first. Never edit source files on master. Even for one-line fixes, use plan-first workflow. Bypasses review, CI gates, and worktree isolation. 26→ 27→**importing time module or calling time.sleep()/datetime.now()** → Read [Universal Tripwires](universal-tripwires.md) first. Use context.time.sleep() and context.time.now() for testability. 28→ 29→**modifying business logic in src/ without adding a test** → Read [Universal Tripwires](universal-tripwires.md) first. Bug fixes require regression tests. 30→
... [19 lines omitted] ...
35→**raising exceptions for expected failure cases** → Read [Universal Tripwires](universal-tripwires.md) first. Use discriminated unions (T | ErrorType) instead. 39→**using bare subprocess.run with check=True** → Read [Universal Tripwires](universal-tripwires.md) first. Use wrapper functions: run_subprocess_with_context() (gateway) or run_with_error_reporting() (CLI).

1→--- 2→title: Gateway Consolidation Checklist 3→last_audited: "2026-02-08 00:00 PT" 4→audit_result: edited 5→read_when: 6→ - "moving a gateway package into the gateway/ directory" 7→ - "extracting a new gateway from existing code" 8→ - "performing large-scale import refactoring across packages" 9→tripwires: 10→ - action: "moving gateway files without git mv" 11→ warning: "Always use git mv to preserve file history. Plain mv + git add loses blame history, making future archaeology harder." 12→ - action: "updating imports one file at a time during gateway consolidation" 13→ warning: "Use LibCST for systematic import updates. Manual editing misses call sites and creates partial migration states. See docs/learned/refactoring/libcst-systematic-imports.md." 14→ - action: "renaming gateway files during a move without checking for non-standard naming" 15→ warning: "Source files that don't follow standard naming (e.g., executor.py instead of abc.py) must be renamed to abc.py/real.py/fake.py during the move. The gateway directory convention requires standard file names." 16→--- 17→ 18→# Gateway Consolidation Checklist 19→ 20→## Context 21→ 22→All gateway packages live under `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/`. This was not always the case — gateways were originally scattered across domain-specific directories (e.g., TUI commands, CLI utilities). Consolidation into a single `gateway/` tree was done because: 23→ 24→1. **Discoverability** — agents searching for "where is the X gateway?" need one place to look, not a codebase-wide search 25→2. **Consistency enforcement** — the 5-file pattern (abc/real/fake/dry_run/printing) is easier to verify when all gateways are siblings 26→3. **Import predictability** — `erk_shared.gateway.<name>.abc` is a mechanical derivation from the gateway name, eliminating guesswork 27→ 28→There are currently 27 gateway packages in this directory. Some use the full 5-file pattern, others use a simplified 3-file pattern (no dry_run/printing). Complex domains (Git, GitHub, Graphite) use nested sub-gateways. 29→ 30→## Operation Ordering: Why It Matters
... [63 lines omitted] ...
51→Moving gateways into sibling directories can surface circular imports that were previously hidden by package boundaries. The symptom is `ImportError: cannot import name 'X' from partially initialized module`. 66→For batch operations, LibCST is nearly mandatory — manual import updates across dozens of files are error-prone and slow. 78→3. **Verify the `__init__.py`** exists in the new directory — missing it produces `ModuleNotFoundError` that looks like a bad import rather than a missing file

1→--- 2→title: Codespace Gateway Pattern 3→read_when: 4→ - "adding a new codespace SSH operation to the gateway" 5→ - "deciding whether a new gateway needs dry-run and printing implementations" 6→ - "testing code that uses os.execvp or NoReturn methods" 7→ - "choosing between exec_ssh_interactive and run_ssh_command" 8→tripwires: 9→ - action: "implementing codespace gateway" 10→ warning: "Use 3-place pattern (abc, real, fake) without dry-run or print implementations." 11→ - action: "adding dry-run or printing implementation for codespace gateway" 12→ warning: "Codespace operations are all-or-nothing remote execution. Dry-run and printing don't apply." 13→ - action: "adding -t flag to run_ssh_command or omitting it from exec_ssh_interactive" 14→ warning: "The -t flag controls TTY allocation. Interactive needs it (rendering); non-interactive breaks with it (buffering). See the two execution modes section." 15→last_audited: "2026-02-08 00:00 PT" 16→audit_result: clean 17→--- 18→ 19→# Codespace Gateway Pattern 20→ 21→The codespace gateway uses a **3-place pattern** (ABC, real, fake) instead of the standard 5-place gateway pattern. This document explains when to omit dry-run and printing implementations — a decision that generalizes to any gateway wrapping process-replacing or inherently remote operations. 22→ 23→## When to Use 3-Place vs 5-Place 24→ 25→The standard gateway has 5 implementations (abc, real, fake, dry_run, printing). The 3-place variant drops dry-run and printing. The deciding question: **can you give the user a meaningful preview of the operation?** 26→ 27→| Characteristic | 5-place (standard) | 3-place (codespace, agent_launcher) | 28→| -------------------------- | -------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------- | 29→| Operations are previewable | Yes — can show "would create branch X" | No — process replacement or remote execution | 30→| Dry-run adds value | Yes — read-only operations still work | No — no local equivalent to "pretend to SSH" |
... [59 lines omitted] ...
44→The gateway exposes two SSH paths that differ in a single flag (`-t` for TTY allocation), but choosing wrong produces hard-to-diagnose failures: 51→**The `-t` flag trap**: `exec_ssh_interactive()` passes `-t` to allocate a pseudo-terminal (required for interactive TUI rendering). `run_ssh_command()` deliberately omits it. Adding `-t` to non-interactive commands causes output buffering issues; omitting it from interactive commands causes terminal rendering failures. Neither failure produces an obvious error message — both manifest as garbled output or hangs. 79→- [Codespace Remote Execution](../erk/codespace-remote-execution.md) — bootstrap wrapper, environment setup, debugging failures

1→--- 2→title: Universal Tripwires 3→last_audited: "2026-02-17 18:30 PT" 4→audit_result: clean 5→read_when: 6→ - "starting any coding task" 7→ - "writing new code in erk" 8→tripwires: 9→ - action: "using bare subprocess.run with check=True" 10→ warning: "Use wrapper functions: run_subprocess_with_context() (gateway) or run_with_error_reporting() (CLI)." 11→ - action: "adding a new method to Git/GitHub/Graphite ABC" 12→ warning: "Must implement in 5 places: abc.py, real.py, fake.py, dry_run.py, printing.py." 13→ - action: "raising exceptions for expected failure cases" 14→ warning: "Use discriminated unions (T | ErrorType) instead." 15→ - action: "using Path.home() directly in production code" 16→ warning: "Use gateway abstractions instead (ClaudeInstallation, ErkInstallation)." 17→ - action: "calling os.chdir() in erk code" 18→ warning: "After os.chdir(), regenerate context using regenerate_context()." 19→ - action: "importing time module or calling time.sleep()/datetime.now()" 20→ warning: "Use context.time.sleep() and context.time.now() for testability." 21→ - action: "adding file I/O or subprocess calls to class `__init__`" 22→ warning: "Keep `__init__` lightweight; use factory methods like `from_config_path()`." 23→ - action: "modifying business logic in src/ without adding a test" 24→ warning: "Bug fixes require regression tests." 25→ - action: "using gh pr diff --name-only in production code" 26→ warning: "For PRs with 300+ files, gh pr diff fails with HTTP 406. Use REST API with pagination instead. See github-cli-limits.md." 27→ - action: "passing session IDs via environment variables" 28→ warning: "Use CLI flags (--session-id) for context propagation, not environment variables. Erk code never has access to CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID." 29→last_audited: "2026-02-16 14:25 PT" 30→audit_result: clean
... [25 lines omitted] ...
45→- [Discriminated Union Error Handling](architecture/discriminated-union-error-handling.md) - Error handling patterns

1→--- 2→title: LibCST Systematic Import Refactoring 3→last_audited: "2026-02-16 02:45 PT" 4→audit_result: clean 5→read_when: 6→ - "refactoring imports across many files" 7→ - "renaming modules or packages" 8→ - "deciding between manual edits and automated refactoring" 9→tripwires: 10→ - action: "manually updating imports across 10+ files" 11→ warning: "Use LibCST via the libcst-refactor agent or a one-off script. Manual editing misses call sites and creates partial migration states." 12→ - action: "writing LibCST transformation logic from scratch" 13→ warning: "The libcst-refactor agent (.claude/agents/libcst-refactor.md) contains battle-tested patterns, gotchas, and a script template. Load it first." 14→ - action: "interleaving file moves and import updates" 15→ warning: "Move ALL files first (git mv), THEN batch-update ALL imports. Interleaving creates intermediate broken states. See gateway-consolidation-checklist.md." 16→ - action: "running targeted edits after replace_all operations in the same file" 17→ warning: "During type migrations, complete all rename operations before attempting targeted edits. replace_all operations change strings that later edits expect to find." 18→ - action: "using replace_all on lines with trailing comments" 19→ warning: "Edit tool's replace_all removes surrounding whitespace, which can collapse lines and cause SyntaxError." 20→ - action: "using replace_all to rename foo to _foo" 21→ warning: "Corrupts existing _foo to __foo. Grep for existing underscored forms before applying replace_all renames." 22→ - action: "completing a libcst-refactor batch rename" 23→ warning: "After bulk rename, grep entire codebase for old symbol name. LibCST leave_Name visitor does NOT rename string literals used as dict keys. Subagent may miss files outside its scope." 24→--- 25→ 26→# LibCST Systematic Import Refactoring 27→ 28→## When to Use LibCST 29→ 30→LibCST parses Python source into a concrete syntax tree that preserves formatting and comments, making it ideal for mechanical import refactoring. The key question is whether the transformation is worth the tooling overhead.
... [357 lines omitted] ...
37→| ABC consolidation (type renames + import moves + field renames) | `libcst-refactor` agent | Multiple transformation types across 40+ files; manual editing is error-prone | 40→**The crossover point is ~10 files.** Below that, the time to write and test a transformer exceeds the time to make manual edits. Above that, manual editing becomes the riskier option because missed call sites cause runtime `ImportError`s that may not surface until a specific code path executes.

1→--- 2→title: Incomplete Command Removal Pattern 3→read_when: 4→ - "removing a workflow command or CLI entry" 5→ - "deprecating or deleting a command from erk" 6→ - "cleaning up dead references after removing a feature" 7→tripwires: 8→ - action: "removing a workflow command or CLI entry" 9→ warning: "Read incomplete-command-removal.md first. Search all string references before removing. String-based dispatch maps like WORKFLOW_COMMAND_MAP aren't caught by type checkers." 10→--- 11→ 12→# Incomplete Command Removal Pattern 13→ 14→When removing a workflow or command, all traces must be removed simultaneously. String-based dispatch maps and configuration references are invisible to type checkers. 15→ 16→## The Problem 17→ 18→Static analysis (ty, mypy, ruff) cannot catch dead references in: 19→ 20→- `WORKFLOW_COMMAND_MAP` string-keyed dictionaries 21→- Workflow YAML filenames 22→- CLI help text and documentation 23→- Slash command files referencing the command 24→- Test fixtures and test data 25→ 26→## Real Example: objective-reconcile (PR #6882) 27→ 28→The `objective-reconcile` command was removed. Traces that needed cleanup: 29→ 30→1. `WORKFLOW_COMMAND_MAP` entry in `constants.py`
... [24 lines omitted] ...

1→--- 2→title: TUI View Switching 3→read_when: 4→ - "adding a new view mode to the TUI" 5→ - "understanding how view switching and caching work" 6→ - "debugging data not appearing in a specific view" 7→ - "working with PlanBodyScreen content type parameterization" 8→tripwires: 9→ - action: "adding a new ViewMode without updating VIEW_CONFIGS" 10→ warning: "Every ViewMode must have a corresponding ViewConfig in VIEW_CONFIGS. Missing configs cause KeyError at runtime." 11→ - action: "using _render() as a method name in Textual widgets" 12→ warning: "Textual's LSP reserves _render(). Use _refresh_display() instead (see ViewBar)." 13→ - action: "pushing PlanBodyScreen without explicit content_type" 14→ warning: "Content type must come from view_mode at push time, not derived inside the screen." 15→ - action: "adding labels to ViewConfig.labels expecting OR semantics from GitHub API" 16→ warning: "GitHub GraphQL uses AND semantics for label arrays. Multiple labels in a single ViewConfig.labels means items must have ALL listed labels. Use separate views for separate label types. See github-graphql-label-semantics.md." 17→ - action: "adding the same label to multiple ViewConfig entries without exclude_labels" 18→ warning: "Without exclude_labels, items matching the shared label appear in multiple views. Use exclude_labels for defense-in-depth deduplication (Plans view excludes erk-learn)." 19→last_audited: "2026-02-17 00:00 PT" 20→audit_result: edited 21→--- 22→ 23→# TUI View Switching 24→ 25→The TUI supports three views — Plans, Learn, and Objectives — with instant switching via keyboard shortcuts. This document explains the view system architecture, the two-tier filtering strategy, and the caching mechanism that enables sub-millisecond view switches. 26→ 27→## Core Types 28→ 29→<!-- Source: src/erk/tui/views/types.py --> 30→
... [120 lines omitted] ...

1→--- 2→title: Review Development Guide 3→last_audited: "2026-02-16 14:20 PT" 4→audit_result: clean 5→read_when: 6→ - creating a new code review for CI 7→ - adding a new review spec to .erk/reviews/ 8→ - understanding the end-to-end review creation process 9→tripwires: 10→ - action: "creating a new review without checking if existing reviews can be extended" 11→ warning: "Before creating a new review, check if an existing review type can handle the new checks. See the review types taxonomy for the decision framework." 12→ - action: "creating a separate GitHub Actions workflow file for a new review" 13→ warning: "Reviews use convention-based discovery from a single workflow. Drop a markdown file in .erk/reviews/ — do NOT create a new .yml workflow." 14→--- 15→ 16→# Review Development Guide 17→ 18→Erk uses a convention-based review system: a single GitHub Actions workflow discovers and runs review specs defined as markdown files. Creating a new review means writing a markdown file, not a workflow file. 19→ 20→## Why Convention-Based (Not Per-Workflow) 21→ 22→Early reviews each had their own `.github/workflows/review-<name>.yml`. This created maintenance problems: duplicated setup steps, inconsistent boilerplate, and no centralized control over tool permissions or timeout defaults. 23→ 24→<!-- Source: .github/workflows/code-reviews.yml --> 25→ 26→The current system uses one workflow (`code-reviews.yml`) that discovers all `*.md` files in `.erk/reviews/`, parses their frontmatter, matches their `paths` patterns against PR changed files, and runs matching reviews as parallel matrix jobs. Adding a review means dropping a markdown file — the workflow handles discovery, path matching, prompt assembly, and Claude invocation automatically. 27→ 28→<!-- Source: src/erk/review/parsing.py, discover_matching_reviews --> 29→<!-- Source: src/erk/review/prompt_assembly.py, assemble_review_prompt --> 30→
... [132 lines omitted] ...
47→This is the most important decision. Creating overlapping reviews wastes CI time and confuses failure attribution.

1→--- 2→title: Erk Glossary 3→last_audited: "2026-02-16 00:00 PT" 4→audit_result: clean 5→read_when: 6→ - "understanding project terminology" 7→ - "confused about domain-specific terms" 8→ - "working with worktrees, plans, or stacks" 9→ - "working with objectives or turns" 10→tripwires: 11→ - action: "parsing objective roadmap PR column status" 12→ warning: "Roadmap table uses separate Plan and PR columns: Plan `#XXXX`=in progress (plan issue), PR `#XXXX`=done (merged PR), both empty=pending. Legacy 4-col format with `plan #XXXX` in PR column is auto-migrated." 13→--- 14→ 15→# Erk Glossary 16→ 17→Definitive terminology reference for the erk project. 18→ 19→**Purpose**: Eliminate confusion about domain-specific terms. When in doubt about terminology, consult this document. 20→ 21→--- 22→ 23→## Core Concepts 24→ 25→### activate.sh 26→ 27→A shell script at `.erk/activate.sh` in each worktree that sets up the development environment when sourced. 28→ 29→**Purpose**: Opt-in shell integration. Users explicitly source this script rather than relying on automatic shell manipulation. 30→
... [1038 lines omitted] ...
543→A sentinel class returned when a PR lookup fails, preserving lookup context (branch name, PR number). Uses `isinstance` check (LBYL) instead of exceptions. 585→- `style: Literal["info", "success", "warning", "error"]` - Visual style hint (default: "info") 737→- `tripwires`: List of action-triggered warnings 748→ warning: "Use .erk/scratch/<session-id>/ instead." 775→**Format**: Defined in doc frontmatter with `action` (pattern to detect) and `warning` (guidance message). 784→ warning: "AI workflow files belong in .erk/scratch/<session-id>/, NOT /tmp/." 862→- `erk land` skips the "not learned from" warning for learn plans (they don't need learning) 968→- **EAFP**: Easier to Ask for Forgiveness than Permission (exception-based error handling) 969→- **LBYL**: Look Before You Leap (check-before-operation error handling) 1033→**Purpose**: Break large, complex goals into reviewable, implementable chunks. Instead of tackling "migrate all errors to Ensure class" in one massive PR, an objective evaluates the codebase, identifies a manageable subset of violations, and creates a plan for that subset.

- [Gateway ABC Implementation Checklist](../architecture/gateway-abc-implementation.md) — the 4-file pattern that fakes implement, including the fake mutation tracking checklist

tool_use.name: Edit
tool_use.param: - [Gateway ABC Implementation Checklist](../architecture/gateway-abc-implementation.md) — The 5-place implementation pattern that fakes are part of
- [Gateway ABC Implementation Checklist](../architecture/gateway-abc-implementation.md) — The 4-place implementation pattern that fakes are part of

tool_use.name: Edit
tool_use.param: **adding a new method to Git/GitHub/Graphite ABC** → Read [Universal Tripwires](universal-tripwires.md) first. Must implement in 5 places: abc.py, real.py, fake.py, dry_run.py, printing.py.
**adding a new method to Git/GitHub/Graphite ABC** → Read [Universal Tripwires](universal-tripwires.md) first. Must implement in 4 places: abc.py, real.py, fake.py, dry_run.py.

tool_use.name: Edit
tool_use.param: 1. **Discoverability** — agents searching for "where is the X gateway?" need one place to ...[truncated 414 chars]...rn (no dry_run/printing). Complex domains (Git, GitHub, Graphite) use nested sub-gateways.

1. **Discoverability** — agents searching for "where is the X gateway?" need one place to ...[truncated 396 chars]...ile pattern (no dry_run). Complex domains (Git, GitHub, Graphite) use nested sub-gateways.

tool_use.name: Edit
tool_use.param: - [Gateway ABC Implementation Checklist](../architecture/gateway-abc-implementation.md) — the 5-place implementation pattern that consolidated gateways must follow
- [Gateway ABC Implementation Checklist](../architecture/gateway-abc-implementation.md) — the 4-place implementation pattern that consolidated gateways must follow

tool_use.param: The codespace gateway uses a **3-place pattern** (ABC, real, fake) instead of the standard...[truncated 468 chars]... 5-place (standard) | 3-place (codespace, agent_launcher) |

tool_use.param: - [Gateway ABC Implementation Checklist](../architecture/gateway-abc-implementation.md) — the 5-place pattern that must be updated during consolidation

tool_use.name: Edit
tool_use.param: **Symmetric gateway API:** `fetch_plan_content()` and `fetch_objective_content()` follow the 5-place gateway pattern (ABC, real, fake, test helper, tests) with matching extraction functions.
**Symmetric gateway API:** `fetch_plan_content()` and `fetch_objective_content()` follow the gateway pattern (ABC, real, fake, test helper, tests) with matching extraction functions.

tool_use.name: Edit
tool_use.param:
### Gateway
An ABC (Abstract Base Class) defining an interface for an external system (Gi...[truncated 33 chars]...-file implementation pattern: `abc.py`, `real.py`, `fake.py`, `dry_run.py`, `printing.py`.

### Gateway
An ABC (Abstract Base Class) defining an interface for an external system (Gi...[truncated 72 chars]....py`, `fake.py` (3-file default), plus `dry_run.py` for dry-run-enabled gateways (4-file).

session.tool_result:
/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/architecture/gateway-removal-pattern.md:**Why 5-place removal:** Partial removal causes type checker errors. If the ABC still declares the property but Real doesn't implement it, `ty` reports an unimplemented abstract method. All layers must change atomically. /home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/architecture/gateway-removal-pattern.md:3. **Layer deletion** — Removed `src/erk/gateway/claude_executor/` containing abc.py, real.py, fake.py, dry_run.py, printing.py /home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/architecture/gateway-specific-patterns.md:- [Gateway ABC Implementation](gateway-abc-implementation.md) - 5-place implementation checklist /home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/architecture/tripwires.md:**removing an abstract method from a gateway ABC** → Read [Gateway ABC Implementation Checklist](gateway-abc-implementation.md) first. Must remove from 5 places simultaneously: abc.py, real.py, fake.py, dry_run.py, printing.py. Partial removal causes type checker errors. Update all call sites to use subgateway property. Verify with grep across packages. /home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/architecture/discriminated-union-error-handling.md:- [Gateway ABC Implementation](gateway-abc-implementation.md) - 5-place implementation pattern for gateway methods /home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/architecture/fake-mutation-tracking.md:- [Gateway ABC Implementation](gateway-abc-implementation.md) - Full 5-place pattern /home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/architecture/gateway-vs-backend.md: warning: "Read gateway-vs-backend.md first. Gateways wrap external tools (5-place: abc, real, fake, dry_run, printing). Backends abstract business logic (3-place: abc, real, fake). Wrong choice creates unnecessary boilerplate or missing test support." /home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/architecture/gateway-vs-backend.md:Gateways wrap external tools and system calls (subprocess, CLI, HTTP). They follow a **5-place implementation pattern**: /home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/architecture/gateway-vs-backend.md:| `printing.py` | Wraps another impl with user-visible output | `gateway/git/printing.py` | /home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/architecture/gateway-vs-backend.md:- `printing.py` enables `--verbose` output without polluting business logic /home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/architecture/gateway-vs-backend.md:**See:** [Gateway ABC Implementation Checklist](gateway-abc-implementation.md) for the full 5-place implementation guide. /home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/architecture/gateway-vs-backend.md:- No `dry_run.py` or `printing.py` — business logic doesn't need these /home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/architecture/gateway-vs-backend.md:| Does it need 5-place implementation? | Yes | No | /home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/architecture/gateway-vs-backend.md:- [Gateway ABC Implementation Checklist](gateway-abc-implementation.md) — 5-place implementation pattern /home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/architecture/prompt-executor-gateway.md:- [Gateway ABC Implementation](gateway-abc-implementation.md) - Full 5-file gateway pattern /home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/architecture/plan-backend-migration.md:- [Gateway vs Backend](gateway-vs-backend.md) - Backend ABC (3-place) vs Gateway ABC (5-place) /home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/architecture/gateway-signature-migration.md:When changing a gateway method signature (especially return types), you must update every implementation and every caller. The gateway's 5-file pattern magnifies the impact—a single method change touches at least 5 files, and typically many more when callers are updated. /home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/architecture/gateway-signature-migration.md:5. **`printing.py`** — Verbose behavior (log and delegate) /home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/architecture/gateway-signature-migration.md:This keeps each PR focused and testable. However, for gateway ABCs where the 5-file pattern already constrains you, doing it atomically in one PR is often cleaner. /home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/architecture/gateway-signature-migration.md:- [Gateway ABC Implementation Checklist](gateway-abc-implementation.md) — The 5-place pattern for all gateway changes /home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/architecture/index.md:- **[github-admin-gateway.md](github-admin-gateway.md)** — working with GitHub repository secrets or admin operations, adding methods to the GitHubAdmin gateway, understanding the 5-place gateway pattern with security-sensitive operations /home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/architecture/flatten-subgateway-pattern.md:<!-- Source: packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/git/printing.py, PrintingGit properties --> /home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/architecture/flatten-subgateway-pattern.md:See properties in `PrintingGit` (`packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/git/printing.py`). Pattern: `return PrintingGitBranchOps(self._wrapped.branch, script_mode=self._script_mode, dry_run=self._dry_run)`. /home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/architecture/http-accelerated-plan-refresh.md:| Printing | `http/printing.py` | Verbose output wrapper | /home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/architecture/http-accelerated-plan-refresh.md:- [Gateway ABC Implementation Checklist](gateway-abc-implementation.md) — 5-place implementation pattern /home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/architecture/erk-architecture.md: │ └── printing.py # Wrapper that logs operations /home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/architecture/gateway-error-boundaries.md: context: "The 5-file gateway pattern has clear error boundary responsibilities. Real implementations catch and convert subprocess/system errors. Fakes simulate errors via constructor params. Dry-run always returns success." /home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/architecture/gateway-error-boundaries.md:In erk's 5-file gateway pattern, **only real.py catches exceptions**. The other four files (abc.py, fake.py, dry_run.py, printing.py) never use try/except — they express failure through different mechanisms. /home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/architecture/gateway-error-boundaries.md:| `printing.py` | Delegates to wrapped implementation (pass-through) | No | /home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/architecture/gateway-error-boundaries.md:### printing.py: Transparent Wrapper
... [11 lines omitted] ...
/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/architecture/gateway-error-boundaries.md:- [Gateway ABC Implementation](gateway-abc-implementation.md) - 5-file checklist covering abc.py, real.py, fake.py, dry_run.py, printing.py /home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/architecture/git-operation-patterns.md:- [Gateway Error Boundaries](gateway-error-boundaries.md) — Where try/except belongs in the 5-file gateway pattern

session.tool_result:
1→--- 2→title: Gateway ABC Implementation Checklist 3→last_audited: "2026-02-15 18:50 PT" 4→audit_result: clean 5→read_when: 6→ - "adding or modifying methods in any gateway ABC interface (Git, GitHub, Graphite)" 7→ - "implementing new gateway operations" 8→ - "composing one gateway inside another (e.g., GitHub composing GitHubIssues)" 9→tripwires: 10→ - action: "creating a new gateway ABC" 11→ warning: "Default is 3-file pattern (abc.py, real.py, fake.py). Only add dry_run.py if the gateway participates in a user-facing --dry-run feature. Most gateways do not." 12→ - action: "adding a new method to a dry-run-enabled gateway ABC (Git, LocalGitHub, Graphite)" 13→ warning: "Must implement in 4 places: abc.py, real.py, fake.py, dry_run.py." 14→ - action: "adding a new method to a 3-file gateway ABC" 15→ warning: "Must implement in 3 places: abc.py, real.py, fake.py." 16→ - action: "removing an abstract method from a gateway ABC" 17→ warning: "Must remove from all implementation files simultaneously (3 or 4 depending on pattern). Partial removal causes type checker errors. Update all call sites. Verify with grep across packages." 18→ - action: "adding subprocess.run or run_subprocess_with_context calls to a gateway real.py file" 19→ warning: "Must add integration tests in tests/integration/test_real_*.py. Real gateway methods with subprocess calls need tests that verify the actual subprocess behavior." 20→ pattern: "subprocess\\.run\\(|run_subprocess_with_context\\(" 21→ - action: "making N sequential gh api calls in a loop when a single GraphQL query could fetch all data" 22→ warning: "Each gh subprocess call costs ~200-300ms overhead. Batch into a single GraphQL query with node fragments when fetching multiple items. See http-accelerated-plan-refresh.md for the dual-path pattern." 23→ - action: "using subprocess.run with git command outside of a gateway" 24→ warning: "Use the Git gateway instead. Direct subprocess calls bypass testability (fakes) and dry-run support. The Git ABC (erk_shared.gateway.git.abc.Git) likely already has a method for this operation. Only use subprocess directly in real.py gateway implementations." 25→ pattern: "subprocess\\.run\\(\\s*\\[.*[\"']git" 26→ - action: "changing gateway return type to discriminated union" 27→ warning: "Verify all implementations import the new types. For 4-file gateways: abc.py, real.py, fake.py, dry_run.py. For 3-file gateways: abc.py, real.py, fake.py." 28→ - action: "designing error handling for a new gateway method" 29→ warning: "Ask: does the caller continue after the failure? If yes, use discriminated union. If all callers terminate, use exceptions. See 'Non-Ideal State Decision Checklist' section." 30→ - action: "adding a new parameter to a gateway ABC method"
... [716 lines omitted] ...
31→ warning: "All implementations must be updated (3 or 4 depending on pattern). Fake may accept but not track new parameters when assertion is not needed for tests." 33→ warning: "Gateway names must reflect the TOOL being wrapped, not the execution mechanism. Use LocalGitHub for gh calls, Git for git calls, CmuxGateway for cmux calls, PromptExecutor for claude calls. A mechanism-named gateway is just moving the mock up one layer without gaining abstraction." 42→**Exception: erk-dev** (`packages/erk-dev/`) is developer tooling and is exempt from these rules. Direct `subprocess.run` with git commands is acceptable in erk-dev since it doesn't need gateway abstractions for its own operations. 106→When designing error handling for a new gateway method, the key question is: **does the caller continue after the failure?** 108→This checklist helps you choose between discriminated unions and exceptions. For full pattern documentation, see [Discriminated Union Error Handling](discriminated-union-error-handling.md). 110→### Decision: Discriminated Union or Exception? 114→| Does at least one caller branch on the error and continue with different logic? | Discriminated union | Exception | 115→| Does the error carry domain-meaningful fields beyond just `message`? | Discriminated union | Exception | 116→| Do all callers terminate on failure (extract message and stop)? | Exception | Discriminated union | 120→- [ ] At least one caller branches on the error and continues with different logic 121→- [ ] Error type carries domain-meaningful fields beyond just `message` 122→- [ ] Error type implements the `NonIdealState` protocol (`error_type` + `message` properties) from `erk_shared.non_ideal_state` 126→### Checklist: Use Exceptions When 128→- [ ] All callers terminate on failure (extract message and stop) 129→- [ ] No caller inspects error type or branches on error content 130→- [ ] Use `UserFacingCliError` at CLI boundary 136→All discriminated union error types must implement the `NonIdealState` protocol from `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/non_ideal_state.py`. The protocol requires two read-only properties: `error_type` (machine-readable classification string) and `message` (human-readable description). 151→**Methods using discriminated unions** (callers branch on error): 155→| `merge_pr` | `MergeResult \| MergeError` | GitHub | 156→| `push_to_remote` | `PushResult \| PushError` | Git remote_ops | 157→| `pull_rebase` | `PullRebaseResult \| PullRebaseError` | Git remote_ops | 159→| `submit_branch` | `SubmitBranchResult \| SubmitBranchError` | BranchManager | 161→**Methods using exceptions** (all callers terminate): 163→| Method | Exception | Why | 165→| Worktree add/remove | `RuntimeError` | No caller inspects error content; all terminate identically | 166→| HTTP operations | `HttpError` | Generic transport failure; callers just surface the message | 170→When changing an existing gateway method's return type (e.g., converting from exception-based to discriminated union), follow this comprehensive migration pattern: 177→ - [ ] `real.py` - Return appropriate error types for failure cases 179→ - [ ] `dry_run.py` - Return appropriate success/error based on mode (4-file gateways only) 181→5. [ ] Update tests to check `isinstance(result, ErrorType)` 186→**PR #6294** (`merge_pr: bool | str` → `MergeResult | MergeError`): 190→- Updated tests to use `isinstance(result, MergeError)` 208→Some resources benefit from existence-check methods that enable Look Before You Leap validation. This pattern prevents cryptic errors when fetching non-existent resources. 216→- When error messages from `get_X()` on missing resources are unclear 239→ user_output(f"Error: Issue #{issue_number} not found") 314→6. **Unit tests** (`tests/unit/`) - Update any tests that assumed failure on missing resource 544→The worktree sub-gateway follows the same 4-file pattern with methods: `list_worktrees()`, `add_worktree()`, `move_worktree()`, `remove_worktree()`, `prune_worktrees()`, `find_worktree_for_branch()`. Note: Worktree operations use exceptions (RuntimeError), not discriminated unions. 631→**Partial removal causes type checker errors** - if you remove from abc.py but forget another implementation, the type checker will complain about missing abstract method implementations. 649→1. Run type checker (`ty`) to verify no abstract method errors 706→├── abc.py # push_to_remote() -> PushResult | PushError 708→├── fake.py # Constructor-injected errors, mutation tracking 714→- **Discriminated union returns**: Methods return `Success | Error` instead of raising 715→- **Error boundary in real.py**: `try/except RuntimeError` converts to `PushError` 716→- **Fake configuration**: `FakeGitRemoteOps(push_to_remote_error=PushError(...))` injects failures 739→- [Gateway Error Boundaries](gateway-error-boundaries.md) - Where exceptions become discriminated unions

tool_use.param: Get list of architecture docs with 5-file references

session.assistant: Let me check the review development guide since it had a specific reference to the "Five-Place Pattern" section which needs attention:
